text.title: feature-prioritization-moscow Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for feature-prioritization-moscow as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Must inflation: 60-80% of items end up categorized as Must Have.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The fail-test is applied loosely. Teams interpret "important" as "must." Without a contrarian pass, no item survives downgrade to Should.

failure-mode.description: No Won't Have category. Teams skip documenting exclusions because it is uncomfortable.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Won't Have is the primary mechanism for preventing scope creep. An empty Won't list means every excluded item is implicitly negotiable, re-entering as "just one more thing."

failure-mode.description: No timebox declared. MoSCoW session runs without a stated capacity number.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without capacity as a constraint, categories reflect desires rather than trade-offs. The 60/20/20 allocation cannot be validated, so the output is a wishlist, not a scoped plan.

failure-mode.description: Treating the 60/20/20 allocation as a law rather than a heuristic, and using it to mechanically assign exact effort budgets.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The allocation is a directional check. Forcing exactly 60% Must effort produces false precision. The check is: "are Must+Should within 80%?" not "is Must exactly 60%?"

failure-mode.description: Changing Must Have items mid-sprint without escalation. PMs add scope by re-categorizing Could items as Must.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Must changes after lockdown destroy the entire value of the exercise. Every Must change must require explicit stakeholder escalation and re-validation of capacity allocation.

failure-mode.description: Using MoSCoW for long-horizon roadmaps (more than one quarter).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: MoSCoW categories are release-scoped. Over a quarter, business context shifts enough that Must items become irrelevant and Won't items become Must. Rerun per release, not per quarter.
